heroEyebrow: UX DESIGNER · TORONTO, CANADA · OPEN TO ROLES
heroCopy: Entry-level UX designer with 16 months of co-op at the Ontario Ministry of Transportation.
lookingFor: Entry-level UX or product design roles · Toronto or hybrid · starting 2026

credibility.label: Co-op at the Ministry of Transportation

howIWorkSteps.title: Listen
howIWorkSteps.desc: Talk to users and stakeholders. Understand the problem before sketching anything.

howIWorkSteps.title: Make
howIWorkSteps.desc: Wireframes to prototypes. Test early and often, with real people.

howIWorkSteps.title: Refine
howIWorkSteps.desc: Synthesize feedback into design decisions backed by evidence.

aboutCopy: UX designer based in Toronto who recently graduated from Wilfrid Laurier University with a BDes, passionate about creating thoughtful, accessible digital experiences that make everyday tasks feel simpler and more intuitive.

whatIDo.title: Research
whatIDo.desc: Semi-structured interviews, usability testing, contextual inquiry, synthesis.

whatIDo.title: Interaction Design
whatIDo.desc: Wireframes, flows, prototypes — desktop and mobile, accessible by default.

whatIDo.title: Service & Systems
whatIDo.desc: Mapping the human side of complex workflows in government and enterprise.

skillsSection.title: Creative Thinking
skillsSection.desc: Approaches problems and challenges in unconventional ways, bringing originality and imaginative solutions.

skillsSection.title: Prototyping
skillsSection.desc: Created models and wireframes to test and refine ideas before final development.

skillsSection.title: Tools
skillsSection.desc: Effectively use Figma, Adobe Illustrator, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel to produce reports, wireframes, and presentations.

skillsSection.title: Creativity
skillsSection.desc: Generating valuable ideas and solutions through imagination and original thinking.

skillsSection.title: Collaboration
skillsSection.desc: Working together towards a common goal using diverse skills and perspectives.

skillsSection.title: Communication
skillsSection.desc: Exchange information, ideas, thoughts, and feelings between individuals and groups.

bio: I'm Kaeleigh — a designer who's happiest when I'm learning something new. UX turned out to be the perfect fit, because every project starts with understanding people I've never met and untangling a problem I haven't solved yet.

Through my degree and a 16-month co-op with the Ontario Ministry of Transportation, I've designed websites, run user research, and shaped complex government and enterprise workflows into clear, accessible flows. That mix of research and hands-on making is where I do my best work.

What drives me is simple: making everyday tasks easier to navigate through thoughtful, human-centred design. And when I'm not designing, you'll find me illustrating, exploring new tools, or hunting for the perfect coffee shop.

testimonials.quote: Kaeleigh did a great job for us. She was dependable and produced quality work. She also stepped up to take on additional leadership responsibilities for our small group when the opportunity presented itself.
testimonials.name: Mike Jones
testimonials.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science, Brigham Young University
